name: Combustion and Flame
subject: chemistry
summary: Burning needs fuel, oxygen, and ignition temperature. Complete vs incomplete combustion — and what's inside a candle flame.

crushed.title: Fire triangle
crushed.text: Fuel + Oxygen + Heat (ignition temp) = Combustion. Remove any one → fire stops.

crushed.title: Complete combustion
crushed.text: Plenty of O₂ → clean blue flame → CO₂ + H₂O. No smoke, max energy.

crushed.title: Incomplete combustion
crushed.text: Limited O₂ → yellow sooty flame → CO (poisonous!) + C (soot) + H₂O.

crushed.title: Candle flame has 3 zones
crushed.text: Dark inner (unburnt vapour) → luminous yellow (incomplete) → blue outer (complete).

crushed.title: Ignition temperature
crushed.text: Minimum temp for a substance to catch fire. Cotton ~250°C, but gunpowder ~80°C.

items.label: Fuel
items.detail: Combustible material — wood, LPG, kerosene, wax

items.label: Oxygen
items.detail: From air (~21% O₂). Fire dies in closed spaces when O₂ runs out

items.label: Heat
items.detail: Must reach ignition temperature to start and sustain combustion

items.label: Inner dark zone
items.detail: Unburnt wax vapour — coolest part, no burning

items.label: Luminous yellow zone
items.detail: Incomplete combustion — carbon glows yellow, produces soot

items.label: Outer blue zone
items.detail: Complete combustion — hottest zone (~1400°C), CO₂ + H₂O formed

demos.title: Complete Combustion of Carbon
demos.description: Carbon burns completely in plenty of oxygen to form carbon dioxide.

steps.label: Heat carbon
steps.description: Carbon is heated to ignition temperature

steps.label: Oxygen supply
steps.description: Plenty of O₂ from air surrounds the carbon

steps.label: Complete burn
steps.description: Blue flame — every carbon atom bonds with O₂

steps.label: CO₂ released
steps.description: Colourless CO₂ gas rises — no soot left behind

demos.title: Incomplete Combustion of Carbon
demos.description: Limited oxygen produces poisonous carbon monoxide and black soot.

steps.label: Limited air
steps.description: Oxygen supply is restricted — e.g. covered flame

steps.label: Yellow flame
steps.description: Incomplete burning — yellow luminous zone appears

steps.label: CO forms
steps.description: Poisonous CO gas produced — odourless and deadly

steps.label: Soot deposits
steps.description: Unburnt carbon deposits as black soot on surfaces

demos.title: Magnesium Ribbon Burning
demos.description: Magnesium burns with a dazzling white light — used in flash photography.

steps.label: Hold in flame
steps.description: Magnesium ribbon is held in a Bunsen burner flame

steps.label: Dazzling flash
steps.description: Intense white light as Mg reacts with O₂

steps.label: White ash
steps.description: White powder (MgO) remains after burning

demos.title: Boiling Water on a Bunsen Burner
demos.description: Zoom from the lab bench to the beaker — watch water heat, bubble, and steam rise.

steps.label: Lab bench
steps.description: Beaker of water on a tripod over a Bunsen burner — wide lab view.

steps.label: Water heats
steps.description: Blue flame transfers heat — convection currents swirl in the water.

steps.label: Bubbles form
steps.description: Zoom in: bubbles nucleate at the bottom and rise as temperature hits 100°C.

steps.label: Rolling boil
steps.description: Vigorous boiling — steam (water vapour) rises from the surface.

flows.title: What Happens Inside a Candle Flame
flows.description: Track wax vapour from the wick through all three flame zones.

steps.label: Wick
steps.description: Heat melts wax near wick; liquid wax rises by capillary action and vapourises.

steps.label: Inner zone
steps.description: Dark zone — wax vapour, no oxygen yet, no combustion.

steps.label: Yellow zone
steps.description: Limited O₂ — carbon particles glow yellow. Soot can form.

steps.label: Blue zone
steps.description: Plenty of O₂ at the edge — CO₂ and H₂O produced. Hottest part.

steps.label: Rising gas
steps.description: Hot products rise — convection current draws fresh O₂ inward.

keyTerms.definition: Chemical process of burning — substance reacts with O₂ releasing heat and light
Minimum temperature at which a substance catches fire
keyTerms.analogy: The temperature threshold before something goes up in flames

keyTerms.definition: Amount of heat produced when 1 kg of fuel burns completely
keyTerms.analogy: Fuel efficiency rating — higher = more energy per kg

chapterSummary.overview: Combustion is a chemical reaction in which a substance burns in oxygen, releasing heat and light. This chapter covers the fire triangle, types of combustion (complete vs incomplete), flame structure, fuel efficiency, and fire safety including how fire extinguishers work.
keyPoints: Fire needs fuel + oxygen + ignition temperature — remove any one to extinguish fire.
Complete combustion (plenty of O₂) → blue flame, CO₂ + H₂O, maximum energy.
Incomplete combustion (limited O₂) → yellow sooty flame, CO (poisonous) + carbon soot.
Candle flame has three zones: dark inner, luminous yellow, non-luminous blue outer.
Different fuels have different calorific values; LPG and CNG are cleaner than coal or wood.
chapterSummary.examFocus: Draw and label flame zones. Compare complete vs incomplete combustion. Explain why CO is dangerous.
